name: Tracker
description: This algorithm is based on the disting EX algorithm of the same name. You may like to review the video on that algorithm, here. The pitch shifter part of the EX algorithm was split out into a separate algorithm on the NT (above), which you can combine with this algorithm if it suits your needs. The algorithm tracks the pitch and envelope of an audio signal, which are output as CVs. It can also generate chords to harmonise with the tracked pitch – either as pitch CVs (to drive VCOs etc.) or as pitch shifter CVs (to drive the Pitch Shifter, to make harmony copies of the tracked audio). The chords can be tuned microtonally using Scala or MTS.
gui_description: The display shows the tracked note at the bottom of the screen, as a MIDI note number plus cents, and as a frequency in Hz. To the right of this is a bar that shows the envelope, and the tuning as a deviation from the true pitch of the tracked note. Above this is the closest note to the tracked note that lies within the current scale (on the left), and the four harmony notes (on the right).

parameters.name: Range
parameters.description: Sets the range of pitches which can be tracked. Set this appropriately for the lowest note that you need to track.

parameters.name: Track bias
parameters.description: An internal parameter of the pitch tracking algorithm, which can help it avoid errors of octave in some circumstances. The default value is recommended in most cases.

parameters.name: Track bias threshold
parameters.description: Sets a signal threshold below which no attempt is made to track pitch.

parameters.name: Harmony mode
parameters.description: Sets the harmony mode – one of 'Shape', 'SATB', or 'MIDI'. The first two are the standard modes described in the polysynth documentation. The last uses MIDI notes provided to the algorithm to directly set the chord notes.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: Sets the MIDI channel on which to receive notes if the harmony mode is 'MIDI'.

parameters.name: Force in tune
parameters.description: Sets whether the chord pitches should be absolutely in tune (allowing expressive pitch bending in a solo part over a straight backing), or in tune with the tracked audio (allowing the generated chords to bend with the tracked audio).

parameters.name: Pitch output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the pitch CV output.

parameters.name: Env output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the envelope CV output.

parameters.name: Change trigger out
parameters.description: The change trigger output bus – emits a trigger pulse when the current tracked note changes.

parameters.name: Change trigger mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the change trigger output.

parameters.name: Chord 1 output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the first chord note CV output.

parameters.name: Chord 2 output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the second chord note CV output.

parameters.name: Chord 3 output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the third chord note CV output.

parameters.name: Chord 4 output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the fourth chord note CV output.

parameters.name: Shift 1 output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the first pitch shifter CV output.

parameters.name: Shift 2 output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the second pitch shifter CV output.

parameters.name: Shift 3 output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above, for the third pitch shifter CV output.
